label: PASS
rationale: Row1 LockOnReset is [0], so the later HardwareReset (type 1) does not relock it; after the manual ReadLocked=0, the stored row1 values are ReadLockEnabled=true, WriteLockEnabled=true, ReadLocked=false, WriteLocked=true, LockOnReset=[0].

label: FAIL
rationale: The target incorrectly reports row1 ReadLocked=true. Row1 was manually set to ReadLocked=false after the PowerCycle, and its [0] LockOnReset does not match HardwareReset, so HardwareReset preserves the false value.

label: PASS
rationale: Row2 was set ReadLockEnabled=false while its stored ReadLocked and WriteLocked values remained true. HardwareReset does not match row2 LockOnReset [0,3], so the stored locks remain true and are correctly returned by Get.

label: FAIL
rationale: The target incorrectly reports row2 ReadLockEnabled=true. The trajectory successfully set row2 column 5 to false, and no later operation re-enabled it; HardwareReset only affects ReadLocked/WriteLocked for matching LockOnReset entries.

label: PASS
rationale: Row3 has an empty LockOnReset set, which means ReadLocked and WriteLocked do not change on any reset. Its stored locks were true after PowerCycle/manual operations, so the Get returning both true is compliant.

label: FAIL
rationale: The target incorrectly clears row3 ReadLocked and WriteLocked to false. With empty LockOnReset, resets do not change those columns, and the preceding stored row3 lock values remain true.

label: PASS
rationale: After HardwareReset, at least one effective lock remains: row1 WriteLockEnabled=true and WriteLocked=true, and row3 also has effective locks. Level 0 Locked therefore must be 1.

label: FAIL
rationale: The target reports Level 0 Locked=0 even though effective locks remain after HardwareReset, including row1 WriteLockEnabled=true and WriteLocked=true. The Level 0 Locked bit is 1 if any range has an enabled locked read or write state.

label: PASS
rationale: The final manual clears remove row1 and row2 WriteLocked and row3 ReadLocked/WriteLocked, while row2 ReadLocked remains ineffective because ReadLockEnabled=false. No enabled locked range remains, so Level 0 Locked=0 is compliant.

label: FAIL
rationale: The target reports Level 0 Locked=1, but the final manual clears leave no effective lock: row1 read/write are false, row2 write is false and read is disabled, and row3 read/write are false. Level 0 Locked must be 0.

label: PASS
rationale: Row2 ReadLockEnabled was set false, but disabling read locking disregards rather than rewrites the stored ReadLocked value. The later effective-lock clear only set row2 WriteLocked=false, so Get correctly returns ReadLocked=true and WriteLocked=false.

rationale: The target incorrectly reports row2 ReadLocked=false. Row2 ReadLockEnabled=false makes the read lock ineffective, but the stored ReadLocked cell remains observable and no step cleared column 7 for row2.

label: PASS

rationale: The target incorrectly leaves row2 WriteLocked=false after TPER_RESET. Programmatic reset matches row2 LockOnReset [0,3], so both ReadLocked and WriteLocked are set to true.
